steps.title: Mark the sentence boundary
steps.content: The words before the blank complete a sentence about geophysicists relying on data about a fault's dimensions and features. The next thought begins with "For the Imperial fault" and explains how the US Geological Survey compiles such data.

steps.title: Reject run-on joins
steps.content: A gives no punctuation between complete thoughts. B uses only a comma before a prepositional phrase, so it does not create a proper boundary. D joins the thoughts awkwardly with "and for." The answer is C.
